explanation: Bypasses the rule by using the built-in Windows tar.exe utility to archive the sensitive files directly. This matches 'selection_path' but evades 'selection_cmd' entirely, because tar.exe is not monitored as a copy tool in the rule logic.

Bypasses the rule by utilizing command-line wildcards to obfuscate the target directory path. This triggers 'selection_cmd' (due to the 'copy ' string) but fails 'selection_path' because the exact literal string '\Google\Chrome\User Data' is never typed out.

Bypasses the rule by using the 'type' command combined with standard output redirection (>) to duplicate the file contents instead of invoking a copy operation. This matches 'selection_path' but safely avoids the 'selection_cmd' parameters.

Bypasses the rule by using PowerShell's native Compress-Archive cmdlet. While the path matches 'selection_path', the cmdlet does not match any of the strings in 'selection_cmd' (such as 'copy-item' or 'cpi'), breaking the 'all of' condition.
